identificationModule.briefTitle: Benefits of Nigella Sativa in Children With Beta Thalassemia Major
identificationModule.officialTitle: Study of The Therapeutic Benefits of Nigella Sativa in Children With Beta Thalassemia Major

descriptionModule.briefSummary: Evaluate the therapeutic effects health benefits, and immunological effect of Nigella sativa in children with beta thalassemia major.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
Study will be carried on children patients with β- thalassemia major attending The Hematology Unit of Pediatric Department at Tanta University Hospital. patients are divided into 2 groups: patients in the control group, patients in the treatment group that will receive nigella sativa powder for 3 consecutive months. Then Evaluate the therapeutic effects health benefits, and immunological effect of Nigella sativa in children with beta thalassemia major.

Evaluate the extent of clinical improvement, reductions in serum iron and ferritin, antioxidant status, treatment of the causes of hemolysis and other therapeutic benefits of Nigella sativa in those patient.

conditions: Nigella Sativa With Beta Thalassemia Major

armGroups.label: patients receiving Nigella
armGroups.description: 40 patients in the treatment group that will receive nigella sativa powder (2 gm/day) for 3 consecutive months.

armGroups.label: patients not received nigella as controls
armGroups.description: 40 patients in the control group will not receive nigella sativa and continued on the usual chelators

interventions.name: nigella sativ
interventions.description: nigella sativa powder (2 gm/day) for 3 consecutive months.

primaryOutcomes.description: nmol serum level of malondialdehyde (nmol per liter)

primaryOutcomes.measure: cluster of differentation4 and 8(CD4 and CD8)
primaryOutcomes.description: CD4 and CD8 cell per cmm

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Thalassemia with iron overload

Exclusion Criteria:

* Thalassemia complicated with diabetes mellitus or hepatitis
* Patients who discontinue treatment

references.citation: Abdelshafy M, El Shanshory M, Shahat K, Al Jaouni SK, Attia M, Hamous SAA, Aboonq MS, Saad AA, Alayoubi AM, Bahashwan AS, Alhussaini AMT, Abdel-Latif HM, Albeihany AM, Hemdan SB, Yousef RS, Aloufi HA, Alsaedi AM, Alsaedi K, Eid RM, Mohamed N, Abdellah WA, Afifi TM, El Sayed SM, Hablas N. Immunological, hematological and biochemical benefits of adjuvant nigella sativa to pharmacotherapy in immune thrombocytopenic purpura patients. Am J Blood Res. 2026 Feb 15;16(1):1-13. doi: 10.62347/DBNQ6080. eCollection 2026.
